other_observers: Mark Peterson, Duane Nelson, and Roger Linfield; I found and identified the bird
duration: off and on for about 6 minutes
specific_location: On ice on northern side of John Martin Res., east of Point Overlook
description: A very large gull, larger than all others gulls nearby (including Herring Gulls (HERG)), the bird's head was white with just a suggestion of brown head streaking. The eyes were small and pale. The bill was large, being thick-based and fairly long, larger in both of these dimensions than were the bills of the nearby HERGs; it was yellow with a red gonydeal spot.  The neck and underparts were white (at least what we could see of them), the back and upper side of the wings were blackish, contrasting only slightly with the black wingtips. I could not see the tail as the bird was standing on the ice mostly facing us, but somewhat to our left.  When it stretched its left wing once, I could see the wingtip pattern: the white tip to p10 was very extensive, extending well up into the area on the feather that most large gulls sport a mirror. There was a sizable mirror on p9, and vague whitish tongues on pp5-7.Behaviors: Standing on ice with Ring-billed Gulls and HERGs.Call: None heardPlumage: Definitive Basic
